codeReview.summary: The feature addresses the reported loop without introducing a correctness regression in scope. `UnprefixedBoardRedirect` now resolves the owning company from the issue identifier or fetched issue record before adding a company prefix (`ui/src/App.tsx:259-286`), while `IssueDetail` preserves the current route scope during UUID->identifier canonicalization via `createScopedIssueDetailPath` and raw router navigation (`ui/src/lib/issueDetailBreadcrumb.ts:64-73`, `ui/src/pages/IssueDetail.tsx:4,828-836`). Focused tests cover both the UUID/company-resolution path and the canonical redirect behavior (`ui/src/lib/unprefixed-board-route.test.ts:43-51`, `ui/src/pages/__tests__/IssueDetail.test.tsx:550-596`). I did not find any blocking or non-blocking code defects in the reviewed change set.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The UI router wrapper auto-prefixes absolute navigations with the active/selected company, which is subtle but directly relevant to route-sync fixes. This feature had to bypass that behavior with raw `react-router-dom` navigation to keep UUID canonicalization from reintroducing a stale company prefix.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `ui/src/lib/router.tsx:62-74` auto-prefixes `useNavigate()` targets via `resolveTo(...)`; the reviewed fix switches `IssueDetail` to `useNavigate as useRawNavigate` and calls `createScopedIssueDetailPath(...)` instead (`ui/src/pages/IssueDetail.tsx:4,21,828-836`). `.factory/library/architecture.md` mentions company-context drift generally but does not record this helper-specific behavior.

summary: Review passed: I checked the mission guidance, feature metadata, handoff, worker transcript skeleton, `web-runtime-worker` skill, and commit `02b6cc7c033e6b5ffb94c72f6933ca8782a93913`. The fix correctly resolves unprefixed issue deep links before company-route sync and preserves canonical issue redirects without reintroducing the stale-prefix loop; I did not find any blocking correctness, regression, or maintainability problems in scope.
